title: Pollination answer with restrained food-systems framing [semantic_mismatch]
question: Why do pollinators matter for food systems? Provide references.
answer: Pollinators matter for food systems because they support the reproduction of many crops and wild plants, helping sustain both agricultural productivity and ecosystem resilience. Policy and assessment reports usually describe their importance in probabilistic terms, noting that dependence varies by crop, region, and management conditions rather than following a single uniform pattern.
citations: Rose S, Borchert O, Mitchell S, Connelly S. Zero Trust Architecture. NIST Special Publication 800-207. 2020.
Kindervag J. No More Chewy Centers: Introducing the Zero Trust Model of Information Security. Forrester Research. 2010.
design_intent: Answer remains unchanged, but citations are replaced with real references from a semantically different domain to test citation-answer coherence.
